max_iv.reason: max_iv=0.30 produced zero buy signals across 3 days and 3361 evaluations since May 8. All previous working configs used 0.65. Historical passes had avg_iv 0.43-0.49. The scanner default ceiling is 0.75. Restoring to 0.65 is the conservative midpoint with documented profitable history.

base_score_threshold.reason: Graded outcomes show 8% win rate at actual hold exits for score bucket 12-14. Efficacy (5-day peak) favors lowering, but graded outcomes are the reliable signal for actual P&L. The effective threshold for most options is 12.0 via iv_rank_gate_score_floor; base_score_threshold only governs low-IV-rank options. Raising from 6.0 to 8.0 tightens this gate without affecting the dominant iv_rank_gate path.

notes: max_iv is the primary blocker: zero signals in 3 days at 0.30. All historical passes had avg_iv 0.43-0.49. Raising to 0.65 restores the productive option universe.
base_score_threshold raise from 6.0 to 8.0 is conservative — iv_rank_gate_score_floor=12.0 is the real filter for most options. This only tightens the low-IV-rank path.
DTE informational: 14-21d options have highest win rate (7.2%); 21-30d drops to 1.7%. Consider tightening expiry_good_max from 45 to 21 in a future calibration once more data is available.
Sentiment and market_structure factors scored 0.0 on all recent passes — verify news-getter feed health.
